diagnosis.final_diagnosis: squamous cell carcinoma of the right lateral anterior tongue (SCCA)
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was supported by the patient's clinical presentation, including an ulcerated lesion on the lateral side of the anterior tongue, histopathological findings indicating invasive, well-differentiated SCCA, and the results of the biopsy. Additionally, imaging studies such as CT and PET/CT were performed, which helped to rule out other primary oral neoplasms and suggested the presence of SCCA.
